message:
feat(twitter): implement full FCP handshake and capability verification

Brings the Twitter connector up to parity with other connectors by
implementing the complete FCP handshake protocol and capability
verification on invoke.

Handshake implementation:
- Parses HandshakeRequest from params instead of ignoring it
- Creates CapabilityVerifier with host's public key, zone, and
  instance ID
- Stores session_id for request correlation
- Converts requested capabilities to CapabilityGrant responses
- Returns full HandshakeResponse with:
  - capabilities_granted list
  - session_id
  - manifest_hash
  - event_caps (streaming=true, replay=false)
  - nonce echo for replay protection

Invoke verification:
- Extracts and validates capability_token from params
- Parses operation ID for verifier
- Extracts resource URIs:
  - twitter:user:{user_id} for user-scoped operations
  - twitter:tweet:{tweet_id} for tweet-scoped operations
- Calls verifier.verify() before dispatching operation
- Returns FcpError::NotConfigured if verifier not set up

New struct fields:
- verifier: Option<CapabilityVerifier>
- session_id: Option<SessionId>

This completes Twitter connector integration with the FCP security
model, enabling capability-constrained access to Twitter operations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
